name: BGC Grand Loop
description: The perfect starter loop for Metro Manila riders. Flat, wide roads through Bonifacio Global City with dedicated bike lanes and scenic city views. Very safe on weekends when roads close to cars.

name: Marikina River Park Trail
description: A long riverside path along the Marikina River. Very flat and shaded in sections. Popular with families and beginner cyclists. Multiple entry and exit points along the route.

name: Valenzuela to Malabon Loop
description: A flat north Manila route through residential and market areas. Great for building base fitness and getting used to traffic riding.
bestTime: Early Sunday mornings for less traffic

name: Baguio Burnham Park Loop
description: Iconic loop around Burnham Park and Session Road area. Cool highland climate, beautiful scenery. Some light rolling hills but nothing extreme. Perfect for tourists visiting Baguio.

name: Tagaytay Windmill Loop
description: The classic Filipino bucket-list climb. Starting from Carmona or Silang, riders ascend to the famous windmills with stunning views of Taal Volcano and Lake. Challenging but incredibly rewarding.

name: Batangas Coastal Route
description: Beautiful coastal road along Batangas Bay. Mix of flat seaside stretches and moderate rolling hills. Ocean breeze makes it more pleasant than inland routes.

name: Cavite Heritage Loop
description: A historical ride through Cavite's heritage towns. Flat to gently rolling terrain passing by Aguinaldo Shrine and Manila Bay coastal roads.

name: Cebu South Coastal Highway
description: Epic Cebu island ride along the south road from Cebu City down to Oslob (whale shark country). Mountainous, challenging, and absolutely stunning. Requires full day commitment.

name: Mactan Island Loop
description: Island loop around Mactan — flat, coastal, and manageable. Pass by the Lapu-Lapu Shrine and seaside resorts. Great introduction to Cebu riding.

name: La Trinidad Strawberry Valley
description: Cordillera mountain riding at its finest. Start in La Trinidad and climb through pine-scented mountain air past vegetable terraces and the famous strawberry farms. A true highland epic.

name: Iloilo Esplanade Promenade
description: Flat, beautiful riverside ride along the Iloilo River esplanade. Iloilo is known as one of the most bike-friendly cities in the Philippines with dedicated cycling infrastructure.
bestTime: Any time — evening is also nice

name: Subic Freeport Zone Loop
description: Ride through the former US naval base, now a sprawling eco-tourism zone. Wide roads, minimal traffic, lush jungle-lined routes, and the ocean backdrop make this a premium riding experience.

name: Davao City Circumferential Route
description: Explore the largest city in the world by land area on two wheels. Mix of flat urban sections and gentle rolling hills through barangays, durian farms, and coastal areas.

name: Naga City River Loop
description: Explore the Bicol region capital on two wheels. Flat riverside roads and provincial routes. The Bicolano hospitality makes this ride especially warm — and the food after is legendary.

name: La Union Surfside Road
description: Bike along the surf coast of La Union. Flat coastal highway with ocean breezes, surf shops, and resorts dotting the route. After riding, reward yourself with surf and a cold drink.

name: Bataan Death March Road
description: A deeply historical and physically demanding ride along the route of the Bataan Death March of WWII. Markers along the way honor fallen heroes. A ride that combines fitness with historical remembrance.
bestTime: 4:30 AM start due to length and heat

name: Clark Freeport Loop
description: Ride through the Clark Special Economic Zone — wide, clean, low-traffic roads in a well-maintained American-era freeport. Flat to very gently rolling. Great for those new to longer rides.

name: Mt. Apo Foothills Gravel Loop
description: Epic MTB-territory ride through the foothills of the Philippines' highest peak. Mix of paved and gravel roads through coffee and pineapple farms with stunning views of Mt. Apo. For experienced riders only.

name: Antipolo Lungsod ng Sining Loop
description: A favorite among Metro Manila cyclists looking for hills without going far. The climb up to Antipolo from Marikina tests your gears and your lungs. City of the Stars view from the top is worth every pedal stroke.

name: Samal Island Loop
description: Take the passenger ferry from Davao to Samal Island and spend a full day circumnavigating this beautiful island paradise. Resort coastlines, jungle hills, and white sand beaches await. Logistics require planning but the payoff is extraordinary.
